title: isodon scrophularioides
intro: Isodon scrophularioides, also known as Himalayan isodon, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the mountain regions of Asia, often found in high-altitude meadows and rocky slopes. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestion. Isodon scrophularioides is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and stems are also used in herbal infusions and decoctions.
